result:
All confirmed as main-thread call sites. I have a complete picture now.

## Shared state between main thread and the `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` worker threads (`src/bio.c`)

Redis actually spawns `BIO_WORKER_NUM` (3) worker threads in `bioInit()`, all running `bioProcessBackgroundJobs`. Here is every piece of state touched by both a worker and the main thread:

| State | Main thread access | Worker thread access | Synchronization |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bio_jobs[worker]` (per-worker `list*` job queue) | `bioSubmitJob()` (`listAddNodeTail`), `bioDrainWorker()` (`listLength`) | `bioProcessBackgroundJobs()` (`listLength`, `listFirst`, `listDelNode`) | `bio_mutex[worker]` (held around every access) |
| `bio_jobs_counter[type]` (pending-job counters) | `bioSubmitJob()` (`++`), `bioPendingJobsOfType()` (read) | `bioProcessBackgroundJobs()` (`--`) | `bio_mutex[worker]` |
| `bio_newjob_cond[worker]` | signaled/waited in `bioSubmitJob`, `bioDrainWorker` | waited/signaled in `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` | Paired with `bio_mutex[worker]` (standard cond-var protocol) |
| `bio_comp_list` (completion-job response list) | `bioPipeReadJobCompList()` (swaps the list, drains it) | `bioProcessBackgroundJobs()` (`listAddNodeTail` for `BIO_COMP_RQ_*` jobs) | `bio_mutex_comp` |
| `job_comp_pipe[0]`/`job_comp_pipe[1]` | reads `job_comp_pipe[0]` in the `ae` event loop (`bioPipeReadJobCompList`) | writes `job_comp_pipe[1]` after appending to `bio_comp_list` | None needed — pipe read/write is kernel-serialized (write ≤ `PIPE_BUF` is atomic); used purely as a wakeup signal, not as a data channel |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_status` | read/written in `aof.c` (`flushAppendOnlyFile`, etc.) and `server.c` (cron/info) | read (`last_status`) + written via `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` in the `BIO_AOF_FSYNC`/`BIO_CLOSE_AOF` branch | `redisAtomic` (C11 `_Atomic`/GCC `__atomic_*` builtins, relaxed ordering) — declared `redisAtomic int` in `server.h:2309` |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_errno` | read via `atomicGet` in `server.c` | written via `atomicSet` in `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` | `redisAtomic` atomics |
| `server.fsynced_reploff_pending` | read/written via `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` in `aof.c` and `server.c` | written via `atomicSet` after a successful AOF fsync | `redisAtomic` atomics |
| `server.bio_cpulist` | set once at config parse time (before `bioInit()` spawns threads); config option is `IMMUTABLE_CONFIG` (`config.c:3230`) | read once at thread start via `redisSetCpuAffinity(server.bio_cpulist)` | None — safe because it is immutable after the threads start (write happens-before thread creation) |

### Not actually cross-thread shared state (checked and ruled out)
- `bio_threads[j]` — written by main thread in `bioInit()`; only ever read/joined/cancelled by the main thread in `bioKillThreads()`. The worker never touches its own slot.
- `bio_worker_title[]` / `bio_job_to_worker[]` — static, read-only after compile-time initialization; never mutated at runtime by either side.
- `bio_job` contents (`fd`, `offset`, flags, `free_fn`, etc.) — allocated by the main thread, handed off via the mutex-protected queue, and exclusively owned/freed by the worker thereafter (classic ownership transfer, not concurrent access).

**Coverage note:** all of this was read directly from `src/bio.c` (single file, in the compile DB) plus a grep sweep of `server.h`/`aof.c`/`server.c`/`config.c`/`evict.c`/`debug.c` for the shared `server.*` fields and public `bio*()` entry points, confirming which side calls what. No LSP blind spots applicable here since it's plain grep/read verification of a single self-contained subsystem.
